title: Python memory promotion report runtime delegate reduction

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Retire the generated memory promotion-report facade delegate to repo_memory_bootstrap.runtime_primitives by expressing the operation as a generated IR function-call step to the existing memory domain report function.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Keep scope bounded to promotion-report wrapper removal; do not claim the memory promotion-report domain logic has moved into generic primitives or that #892 is complete.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Bounded decomposition, touched-path narrowing, validation tightening, and plan-local residue routing.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.
canonical_core.next_action: Update the memory promotion-report operation contract to call repo_memory_bootstrap.installer.promotion_report through python.function.call, remove the obsolete operation-executor handler, regenerate outputs, and prove conformance.
completion_criteria: memory.promotion-report.report operation IR uses python.function.call instead of memory.promotion_report.load.
generated memory operation executor no longer defines or routes _handle_memory_promotion_report_load.
generated memory runtime facade no longer exports _load_memory_promotion_report.
The slice is validated and closed out without claiming the domain promotion-report implementation is generic primitive coverage.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: Create a bounded plan for Python memory promotion report runtime delegate reduction.
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: For this bounded slice, memory.promotion-report.report uses python.function.call, the generated operation executor no longer contains _handle_memory_promotion_report_load, the generated memory runtime no longer exports _load_memory_promotion_report, and static proof rejects treating the call as portable primitive coverage. The larger #892 epic remains open.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: retired the memory promotion-report runtime facade delegate while keeping the remaining package-domain report function explicit as python.function.call external-adapter debt

closure_check.accepted values: Accepted values: slice status = complete|completed|bounded slice complete; larger-intent status = closed|complete|completed for archive-and-close or open|partial|unfinished for archive-but-keep-lane-open; closure decision = archive-and-close|archive-but-keep-lane-open. Prefer `agentic-planning archive-plan <plan> --prepare-closeout` before hand-editing closeout fields.
closure_check.why this decision is honest: The slice removes one obsolete generated runtime facade hop but does not make promotion-report portable and does not complete #892.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: python.function.call is explicitly inventoried as external-adapter debt; stale delegate names are absent; generated package, conformance, memory, and Docker proofs passed.
closure_check.reopen trigger: A future proof finds memory.promotion_report.load or _load_memory_promotion_report reintroduced, or promotion-report is claimed as portable primitive coverage without replacing the package-domain function.

improvement_signal_review.guidance: At closeout, separate acted-on, reported-only/routed, and dismissed incidental findings; keep this compact and evidence-backed.
signals found: Static proof needed an explicit external_adapter_note for python.function.call entries so domain-runtime function calls cannot be mistaken for portable primitive coverage.
signals fixed: Added external_adapter_note to the promotion-report inventory entry and kept the static proof guard.

continuation: Continue #892 by shrinking the next generated memory/planning/workspace source runtime delegate while keeping product-domain boundaries explicit.
